examples.user_query: Copy SNMP credentials from device 172.16.122.190 to devices 172.16.122.191 and 172.16.122.192
examples.notes: User says 'SNMP credentials' without specifying read/write → copy both snmp read and write. cli=false because no CLI/SSH mention.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SNMP credentials from 172.16.122.190 to 2 devices

examples.user_query: Show me the authentication settings for router ROU-001
examples.expected_response: Retrieved credentials for ROU-001: SNMP v3 enabled, CLI configured with SSH on port 22

examples.user_query: Update SNMP v3 community for all switches in building A with username admin123
examples.expected_response: Successfully updated SNMP v3 credentials for 3 switches in building A

examples.user_query: Change the CLI password for device 172.16.122.190

pre_confirmation.message: This will update CLI password for device 172.16.122.190. Please provide the new password.

examples.expected_response: Successfully updated CLI password for device 172.16.122.190

examples.user_query: Delete credentials for decommissioned devices DEV-001, DEV-002, and DEV-003

pre_confirmation.message: Are you sure you want to delete credentials for 3 devices? This action cannot be undone.

examples.expected_response: Successfully deleted credentials for 3 devices

examples.user_query: List all devices that have credentials configured
examples.expected_response: Found 25 devices with configured credentials: [list of devices]

examples.user_query: What are the default SNMP settings?
examples.expected_response: Default SNMP settings: Version 2c, Port 161, Community: public

examples.user_query: Trust the SSH fingerprint for devices in the DMZ zone
examples.expected_response: Successfully trusted SSH fingerprints for 3 devices in DMZ zone

examples.user_query: Get the HTTPS certificate for router 192.168.1.1
examples.expected_response: Retrieved HTTPS certificate for 192.168.1.1: Issued by CA, Expires on 2027-01-15

examples.user_query: What is the HTTPS port configured for switch SW-MAIN-001?
examples.expected_response: HTTPS port for SW-MAIN-001 is 443

examples.user_query: Configure SNMP v2c with community string 'private123' for device RT-CORE-01
examples.expected_response: Successfully configured SNMP v2c for RT-CORE-01

examples.user_query: Revoke trust for devices that failed security audit
examples.expected_response: Successfully revoked trust for 2 devices

examples.user_query: Decrypt and show me this password: enc_abc123xyz
examples.security_warning: This operation requires valid license and will be audited
examples.expected_response: Decrypted password: ********

examples.user_query: What permissions do I have for credential management?
examples.expected_response: Your access level: EDIT - You can view and modify device credentials

examples.user_query: Replicate all authentication settings from main-router to backup-router-01 and backup-router-02
examples.notes: User says 'all authentication settings' → copy everything: snmpRead=true, snmpWrite=true, cli=true
examples.expected_response: Successfully replicated all credentials from main-router to 2 backup routers

examples.user_query: Copy CLI device credentials from scalance device to ruggedcom device
examples.notes: CLI/SSH only. scalance and ruggedcom are Siemens device brand names used as device IDs. snmpRead and snmpWrite MUST be false.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied CLI credentials from scalance to ruggedcom

examples.user_query: Copy SSH credentials from scalance to ruggedcom
examples.notes: SSH = CLI credential type. snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false, cli=true
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SSH credentials from scalance to ruggedcom

examples.user_query: Transfer CLI authentication from SCALANCE-X200 to RUGGEDCOM-RS900
examples.notes: CLI only transfer. Device names are Siemens product names. snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false, cli=true
examples.expected_response: Successfully transferred CLI credentials from SCALANCE-X200 to RUGGEDCOM-RS900

examples.user_query: Copy only SSH settings from device-001 to device-002 and device-003
examples.notes: SSH is CLI. User says 'only SSH' so snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false, cli=true
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SSH credentials from device-001 to 2 devices

examples.user_query: Replicate command line credentials from router-A to router-B
examples.notes: command line = CLI. snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false, cli=true
examples.expected_response: Successfully replicated command line credentials from router-A to router-B

examples.user_query: Copy SNMP read credentials from scalance to ruggedcom
examples.notes: SNMP read only. Even though devices are scalance/ruggedcom, credential type is SNMP read. cli=false.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SNMP read credentials from scalance to ruggedcom

examples.user_query: Copy SNMP read community from device-001 to device-002
examples.notes: SNMP read community = snmpRead only. snmpWrite=false, cli=false.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SNMP read community from device-001 to device-002

examples.user_query: Copy SNMP write community from switch-001 to switch-002 and switch-003
examples.notes: SNMP write community only. snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=true, cli=false.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SNMP write community from switch-001 to 2 devices

examples.user_query: Copy SNMP and CLI credentials from FW-001 to FW-002
examples.notes: Both SNMP (read+write) and CLI requested. snmpRead=true, snmpWrite=true, cli=true.
examples.expected_response: Successfully copied SNMP and CLI credentials from FW-001 to FW-002

examples.user_query: Duplicate SSH access settings from scalance-xc200 to multiple ruggedcom devices
examples.notes: SSH = CLI only. Multiple destinations. snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false, cli=true.
examples.expected_response: Successfully duplicated SSH credentials from scalance-xc200 to 2 ruggedcom devices

examples.user_query: Set SSH port to 2222 and enable NETCONF on port 830 for all edge routers
examples.expected_response: Successfully updated CLI ports for 3 edge routers

examples.user_query: Remove all credentials for test environment devices

pre_confirmation.message: This will delete credentials for all test environment devices. Continue?

examples.expected_response: Successfully deleted credentials for 4 test environment devices

examples.user_query: Configure SNMP v3 with SHA authentication and AES encryption for firewall FW-001
examples.expected_response: Successfully configured SNMPv3 with SHA/AES for firewall FW-001

examples.user_query: Show credentials for IP address 10.20.30.40
examples.expected_response: Credentials for 10.20.30.40: SNMP v2c configured, CLI via SSH port 22

examples.user_query: Update community string to 'monitoring2024' for all SNMP v2 devices in datacenter
examples.expected_response: Successfully updated SNMP community string for 3 datacenter devices

clarification_needed: Which device(s) would you like to update?
What type of credentials? (SNMP, CLI/SSH, or both)
What changes do you want to make?
Which device should I copy credentials FROM?
Which device(s) should I copy credentials TO?
Which credential types? (SNMP read, SNMP write, CLI/SSH)

ambiguous_query_examples.user_query: Copy credentials from scalance to ruggedcom
clarification_needed: Which credential types should I copy? (SNMP read, SNMP write, CLI/SSH, or all?)
ambiguous_query_examples.notes: Source and destination are clear. Only credential type is ambiguous. If user says CLI or SSH → cli=true, snmpRead=false, snmpWrite=false.

clarification_needed: Or would you like the default SNMP settings?

error_recovery_examples.user_query: Copy credentials from DEV-999 to DEV-001
error_recovery_examples.error_message: Source device DEV-999 not found in system
recovery_suggestions: Would you like to see a list of available devices?
Did you mean DEV-099 or DEV-998?
You can use the command 'list all devices' to find the correct device ID

error_recovery_examples.user_query: Update SNMP for device without proper permissions
error_recovery_examples.error_message: You don't have EDIT permission for credential repository
recovery_suggestions: Your current permission level is VIEW only
Please contact your administrator to request EDIT access
You can check your permissions with 'what are my permissions'

error_recovery_examples.error_message: Port number must be between 1 and 65535
recovery_suggestions: Please provide a valid port number between 1 and 65535
